title: Access Controls
description: Technical policies and procedures to allow access only to authorized persons to electronic protected health information (ePHI).

title: Audit Controls
description: Hardware, software, and procedural mechanisms to record and examine activity in systems that contain or use ePHI.

title: Integrity Controls
description: Policies and procedures to protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction, including electronic mechanisms to corroborate data integrity.

title: Transmission Security
description: Technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to ePHI transmitted over electronic communications networks.

title: Encryption at Rest
description: Mechanisms to encrypt ePHI stored in databases, file systems, and backups to prevent unauthorized disclosure.

title: Minimum Necessary Standard
description: Policies ensuring that only the minimum necessary amount of PHI is used, disclosed, or requested for a given purpose.

title: Business Associate Agreements
description: Written contracts with third-party vendors ensuring they safeguard PHI and comply with HIPAA requirements.

title: Breach Notification
description: Procedures to detect, report, and notify affected individuals and HHS of breaches of unsecured PHI within required timeframes.

title: Risk Analysis and Management
description: Conduct accurate and thorough assessments of potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI.

title: Workforce Training
description: Training programs for all workforce members on policies and procedures regarding PHI handling, security awareness, and incident reporting.
